steps.title: Check current lima setup
steps.description:
Verify your lima version and configuration:
```bash
limactl --version
```
This feature requires a working lima installation.

steps.title: Review lima configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant lima configuration:
Hello. I would like to give some ideas and advices about filesystem sharing.

So, since we are using QEMU, lets see which options do we have:
- VirtioFS. Looking very cool, seems to have really good performance, but works only on Linux hosts.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Filesystem Sharing
steps.description:
The guestagent will soon require root privileges (mostly for virtio stuff), so the guestagent will have to run as the root.
```yaml
The error is happening because the vport device somehow wires up both `write_iter` and `write`: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/v5.11/fs/read_write.c#L540-L545

I guess I have to inject some pipe as a "shim" fd, or use ssh instead of virtserial.


virtserial seems slower than ssh :(


### virtserial
qemu flags: `-device virtio-serial -chardev socket,id=foo,path=foo.sock,server,nowait -device virtserialport,chardev=foo.sock,name=foo`
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Filesystem Sharing" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The guestagent will soon require root privileges (mostly for virtio stuff), so the guestagent will have to run as the root.
codeSnippets:
The error is happening because the vport device somehow wires up both `write_iter` and `write`: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/v5.11/fs/read_write.c#L540-L545

I guess I have to inject some pipe as a "shim" fd, or use ssh instead of virtserial.


virtserial seems slower than ssh :(


### virtserial
qemu flags: `-device virtio-serial -chardev socket,id=foo,path=foo.sock,server,nowait -device virtserialport,chardev=foo.sock,name=foo`

2) Set up a ssh "sync"
`mutagen sync create /tmp/test lima-default:/tmp/test`

With some arbitrary mapping of the mutagen YAML configuration:

https://mutagen.io/documentation/introduction/configuration#configuration-files

prerequisites.description: A working lima installation or development environment.
